title: My Purpose Unfolds as I Move
subtitle: An affirmation for moving before clarity arrives
excerpt: An affirmation for the action-first model of purpose: you do not have to know your purpose before you begin. The purpose reveals itself in the moving. This statement is for the days when waiting for certainty has become the obstacle.

practice.whenToUse: Use when waiting for purpose to become clear before acting has become the pattern, or on mornings when the lack of a defined direction feels like a reason not to move.

content.text: An affirmation for the action-first model of purpose. Clarity rarely arrives before the first step. It tends to arrive in the process of taking it. This statement counters the paralysis of waiting for purpose to be clear before beginning to move toward it.

Purpose is not usually a fixed thing waiting to be located. It is something that builds through action: through the noticing of what you keep returning to, what you find yourself caring about, what direction feels more alive than others. The moving comes first. The clarity builds from the moving.
My purpose unfolds as I move.

Say it in the morning before you start, particularly on days when the lack of a clear direction feels like a reason to stay still. Then move toward whatever is in front of you. The direction will become clearer in the doing.
